title: Network Evidence, Safety, and Troubleshooting Method · Mastery Check
summary: Operate a safe virtual help-desk lab with tickets, diagrams, baselines, commands, packet evidence, and change records. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a reproducible network evidence notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply identify, theorize, test, plan, implement or escalate, verify, prevent, and document steps to one network incident.
Separate user report, observed symptom, affected scope, recent change, and reproducible condition before forming a theory.
Form one falsifiable probable-cause theory and isolate it with bottom-up, top-down, or divide-and-conquer evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Net Troubleshooting Method evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Net Troubleshooting Method assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Apply identify, theorize, test, plan, implement or escalate, verify, prevent, and document steps to one network incident. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network evidence method. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Troubleshooting Method, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a reproducible network evidence notebook while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Trying the most common fix before recording symptoms is the fastest reliable method. This fails the Net Troubleshooting Method contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Zero Trust Architecture (NIST SP 800-207) — Controls zero-trust terminology, policy decision, trust, identity, and least-privilege coverage.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply identify, theorize, test, plan, implement or escalate, verify, prevent, and document steps to one network incident.

hints: Name the Net Troubleshooting Method precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: network evidence method. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Trying the most common fix before recording symptoms is the fastest reliable method.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Incident Documentation applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record timeline, environment, commands, findings, actions, outcomes, lessons, and evidence without exposing secrets.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
